title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Authentic Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Perfection
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Lamb Ouzi. This whole slow roasted Middle Eastern lamb dish is perfect for special occasions. Get the best Lamb Ouzi recipe with step-by-step instructions.
seo.h1: Authentic Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Lamb
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Lamb Ouzi
Step-by-Step Instructions for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
Pro Tips for the Best Lamb Ouzi Results
Variations and Substitutions for Lamb Ouzi
seo.imageAlt: A beautifully presented whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi, garnished with nuts and herbs, ready to serve for a Middle Eastern feast.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich flavors of this authentic Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi, slow-roasted to perfection and served with aromatic rice.

description: Experience the grandeur of a traditional Middle Eastern feast with this whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi. Tender, fall-off-the-bone lamb is infused with aromatic spices, making it a truly spectacular centerpiece for any special occasion. Served atop a bed of fragrant ouzi rice, this dish promises an unforgettable culinary journey.

ingredients.item: Whole lamb shoulder or leg, boneless

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb Marinade
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine olive oil, minced garlic, lemon juice, baharat spice mix, ground cumin, ground coriander, paprika, turmeric, black pepper, and salt. Mix well to form a paste.

instructions.title: Marinate the Lamb
instructions.text: Score the entire surface of the lamb shoulder or leg with deep incisions. Rub the prepared marinade generously all over the lamb, ensuring it penetrates into the scores. Place the lamb in a large dish, cover tightly, and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, to allow the flavors to infuse.

instructions.title: Slow Roast the Lamb
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 300°F (150°C). Transfer the marinated lamb to a large roasting pan. Pour 1 cup of water or lamb broth into the bottom of the pan. Cover the pan tightly with aluminum foil to create a sealed environment. Roast for 3.5 to 4 hours, or until the lamb is incredibly tender and easily pulls apart with a fork. For the last 30 minutes, remove the foil to allow the lamb to brown slightly. Once cooked, remove from the oven and let the lamb rest, covered, for 20 minutes before shredding or slicing.

instructions.title: Prepare the Ouzi Rice Base
instructions.text: While the lamb is resting, rinse the basmati rice under cold water until the water runs clear. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat a tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat. Add the diced carrots and sauté for 3-5 minutes until slightly softened. Stir in the rinsed rice, frozen peas, and a pinch of baharat.

instructions.title: Cook the Ouzi Rice
instructions.text: Pour in the lamb broth (or water) over the rice mixture. Bring the liquid to a boil, then immediately reduce the heat to low. Cover the pot tightly with a lid and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is fluffy. Remove from heat and let it sit, covered, for another 5 minutes before fluffing with a fork.

instructions.title: Assemble and Serve
instructions.text: Gently shred or slice the slow-roasted lamb. On a large, festive serving platter, spread the aromatic ouzi rice evenly. Arrange the tender lamb pieces over the rice. Generously garnish with toasted slivered almonds, pine nuts, golden raisins, and freshly chopped parsley or mint. Serve hot and enjoy this magnificent Middle Eastern feast.

tips: For truly tender lamb, ensure your roasting pan is tightly sealed with foil to trap moisture and steam the meat effectively during the initial cooking phase.
While baharat is traditional, you can customize the spice blend with additional cardamom, cinnamon, or cloves for a richer aroma in both the lamb and the rice.
Leftover Lamb Ouzi can be stored in an airtight container for up to 3 days. Reheat gently in the oven or microwave, adding a splash of broth to the rice to keep it moist.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of the Middle East with a dish that embodies hospitality, flavor, and tradition: Lamb Ouzi. This magnificent creation, featuring whole slow-roasted lamb served with aromatic, spiced rice and often garnished with toasted nuts, is more than just a meal; it's a celebration. Perfect for special occasions, family gatherings, or simply when you crave an extraordinary feast, the process of preparing a whole slow-roasted Lamb Ouzi is a labor of love that yields incredibly tender meat and deeply flavorful rice. Forget dry, tough lamb; this Lamb Ouzi recipe guarantees a succulent, fall-off-the-bone experience that will leave your guests utterly impressed.

## What is Lamb Ouzi?

Lamb Ouzi, sometimes spelled "Ozzy" or "Ozi," is a quintessential Middle Eastern dish renowned for its grandeur and deliciousness. While regional variations exist, the core concept involves slow-cooking a large cut of lamb (often a leg, shoulder, or even a whole small lamb) until it's exceptionally tender, then serving it with a rich, spiced rice pilaf. The rice, typically Basmati, is infused with a medley of aromatic spices like Baharat, cinnamon, cardamom, and allspice, and often mixed with minced meat, peas, carrots, or chickpeas. The "whole slow roasted" aspect refers to the traditional method of cooking the lamb low and slow, allowing its natural fats to render and its connective tissues to break down, resulting in unparalleled tenderness and depth of flavor. This dish is a centerpiece at weddings, Eid celebrations, and other significant events, symbolizing abundance and generosity.

## Ingredients You Need for Lamb Ouzi

Crafting the perfect whole slow-roasted Lamb Ouzi requires a thoughtful selection of fresh, high-quality ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need for this magnificent Lamb Ouzi recipe:

For the Lamb:
*   1 whole lamb shoulder or leg (5-7 lbs/2.5-3.5 kg), bone-in preferred
*   4-5 cloves garlic, minced
*   1 large onion, roughly chopped
*   1/4 cup olive oil
*   2 tablespoons Baharat spice blend (or a mix of allspice, black pepper, cinnamon, nutmeg, cardamom)
*   1 tablespoon ground cinnamon
*   1 teaspoon ground cardamom
*   1 teaspoon black pepper
*   2 teaspoons salt (or to taste)
*   1 teaspoon dried thyme (optional)
*   4-5 cups hot water or lamb/beef broth

For the Spiced Rice:
*   3 cups Basmati rice, rinsed and soaked for 30 minutes
*   2 tablespoons ghee or unsalted butter
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   500g ground beef or lamb (optional, but traditional)
*   1 tablespoon Baharat spice blend
*   1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cardamom
*   1/2 teaspoon black pepper
*   1 teaspoon salt (or to taste)
*   4 cups hot lamb/beef broth or water
*   1 cup frozen peas (optional)
*   1/2 cup chopped carrots (optional)

For Garnish:
*   1/2 cup pine nuts, lightly toasted
*   1/2 cup slivered almonds, lightly toasted
*   Fresh parsley, chopped, for garnish

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

Mastering this Lamb Ouzi recipe is a rewarding culinary achievement. Follow these steps for a show-stopping result:

1.  **Prepare the Lamb:** Pat the lamb shoulder/leg dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, make several deep incisions all over the lamb. In a small bowl, mix the minced garlic, olive oil, Baharat, cinnamon, cardamom, black pepper, salt, and thyme. Rub this marinade generously all over the lamb, ensuring it penetrates the incisions. Place the chopped onion in the bottom of a large roasting pan. Place the marinated lamb on top of the onions.
2.  **Slow Roast the Lamb:** Preheat your oven to 300°F (150°C). Pour 2 cups of hot water or broth into the bottom of the roasting pan, around the lamb (not over it). Cover the roasting pan tightly with foil or a lid. Roast for 4-5 hours, or until the lamb is fork-tender and practically falling off the bone. Halfway through cooking, add another 2-3 cups of hot water/broth to the pan to keep the lamb moist and prevent drying. For the last 30 minutes, remove the foil and increase the oven temperature to 375°F (190°C) to allow the lamb to brown and crisp slightly. Once cooked, remove the lamb from the oven, transfer it to a large serving platter, and tent loosely with foil. Let it rest for at least 30 minutes. Strain the pan juices and reserve for basting or serving.
3.  **Prepare the Spiced Rice:** While the lamb rests, drain the soaked Basmati rice. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat ghee or butter over medium heat. Sauté the finely chopped onion until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. If using, add the ground meat and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned. Drain any excess fat. Stir in the Baharat, cinnamon, cardamom, black pepper, and salt. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
4.  Add the drained Basmati rice to the pot and stir gently to coat the grains with the spices and aromatics. Pour in the hot broth or water. If using, add frozen peas and chopped carrots. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender. Do not lift the lid during cooking. Once cooked, remove from heat and let stand, covered, for another 10 minutes. Fluff gently with a fork.
5.  **Assemble and Garnish:** On your large serving platter, arrange the fluffed spiced rice around the whole slow-roasted lamb. Drizzle some of the reserved pan juices over the lamb and rice. Garnish generously with toasted pine nuts, slivered almonds, and fresh chopped parsley.

## Pro Tips for the Best Lamb Ouzi Results

To elevate your Lamb Ouzi recipe from great to unforgettable, consider these expert tips:

*   **Marination is Key:** For maximum flavor and tenderness, marinate the lamb overnight in the refrigerator. The longer it sits, the more the flavors penetrate the meat.
*   **Low and Slow is the Way to Go:** Resist the urge to rush the roasting process. The low temperature and long cooking time are crucial for breaking down tough fibers and rendering fat, resulting in incredibly tender, juicy lamb.
*   **Rest Your Meat:** Always allow the lamb to rest after roasting. This crucial step allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring it remains moist and flavorful when carved.
*   **Quality Spices Matter:** Use fresh, high-quality spices. If possible, grind your own Baharat blend for an unparalleled aromatic experience.
*   **Don't Skip Soaking the Rice:** Soaking Basmati rice helps remove excess starch, resulting in fluffier, more distinct grains.
*   **Toast Your Nuts:** Toasting pine nuts and almonds brings out their natural oils and enhances their nutty flavor, adding a delightful crunch to the final dish.

## Serving Suggestions for Lamb Ouzi

Lamb Ouzi is a complete meal in itself, but it truly shines when accompanied by a few complementary side dishes that balance its richness.

*   **Fresh Salads:** A crisp, vibrant salad like Fattoush (with toasted pita bread, mixed greens, and a sumac dressing) or Tabbouleh (a parsley and bulgur wheat salad) provides a refreshing counterpoint to the rich lamb and rice.
*   **Yogurt Sauce:** A simple cucumber and mint yogurt sauce (like a mild tzatziki or raita) or a bowl of creamy labneh can offer a cooling element that cuts through the richness of the meat.
*   **Pickles:** A selection of Middle Eastern pickles (like pickled turnips or cucumbers) adds a tangy, acidic crunch that complements the savory flavors.
*   **Warm Bread:** Fresh pita bread or other flatbreads are perfect for scooping up any extra rice or tender lamb pieces.
*   **Roasted Vegetables:** Simple roasted vegetables such as bell peppers, zucchini, or eggplant can be roasted alongside the lamb or prepared separately.

## Variations and Substitutions for Lamb Ouzi

While the traditional whole slow-roasted Lamb Ouzi is a masterpiece, there are several ways to adapt this Lamb Ouzi recipe to suit different preferences or dietary needs:

*   **Smaller Cuts of Lamb:** If a whole shoulder or leg is too much, you can use smaller cuts like lamb shanks or a boneless leg of lamb. Adjust cooking times accordingly.
*   **Stuffed Lamb:** Some variations involve partially deboning the lamb and stuffing it with a portion of the spiced rice mixture before roasting, creating a dramatic presentation when carved.
*   **Rice Fillings:** Experiment with adding other ingredients to your spiced rice. Chickpeas, dried apricots, or even thinly sliced dates can add different textures and flavors.
*   **Vegetarian Ouzi:** For a vegetarian version, omit the lamb entirely. Create a hearty, spiced rice pilaf with plenty of vegetables, chickpeas, lentils, and dried fruits. You can serve this alongside roasted halloumi or a rich vegetable stew.
*   **Spice Adjustments:** Feel free to tweak the spice levels. Add a pinch of dried rose petals or a hint of saffron to the rice for an even more luxurious aroma and flavor.
*   **Individual Portions:** For a more manageable serving, you can prepare individual Lamb Ouzi parcels by wrapping portions of the spiced rice and shredded lamb in phyllo pastry or large cabbage leaves and baking them.

Preparing a whole slow-roasted Lamb Ouzi is an unforgettable culinary experience, a testament to the rich and diverse flavors of Middle Eastern cuisine. This impressive Lamb Ouzi recipe, with its tender, aromatic lamb and fragrant spiced rice, is guaranteed to be the star of any gathering. While it requires patience and a little planning, the magnificent results – a dish that's as beautiful to behold as it is delicious to eat – are well worth the effort. Dive into this timeless tradition and bring the warmth and hospitality of the Middle East to your dining table.
